name: Diclofenac
executiveSummary: Non-narcotic NSAID with analgesic, antipyretic, and anti-inflammatory action; IV use according to institutional prescription.
indications:
## Indications

- Moderate to severe acute pain.
- Inflammation in selected contexts.

## Precautions

- Risk of gastrointestinal and renal effects. Restricted use in pediatrics according to protocol.

> Adjust per institutional protocol and medical prescription.

pediatrico.infusionRate: From 30 min to 2 h via syringe pump.
pediatrico.notes: Analgesic, antipyretic, non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID), non-narcotic. May cause dizziness, headache, rash, abdominal pain, diarrhea.

stability:
## Pediatric chart

- Discard remainder once opened.
adverseEffects:
## Effects

- Dizziness, headache, rash, abdominal pain, diarrhea, elevated transaminases.

bibliography.citation: Institutional pediatric dilution and administration guide. June 2026.
ANMAT. Drug information and authorized prescribing information in Argentina.
American Academy of Pediatrics. Medication guidance in critical care.
Argentine Society of Infectious Diseases (SADI). Guidelines and consensus statements.
